description_long: This is a double-headed axe with a symmetrical design, featuring two large crescent-shaped blades mounted on either side of a central wooden handle. The left blade is metallic silver, while the right blade is a reddish-orange color with visible speckling, suggesting either paint or surface wear. The handle is cylindrical, appears to be made of wood, and is segmented with red bands, possibly for grip or decorative purposes. The blades are broad and flat, with sharp outer edges and a smooth, continuous curve, lacking any beard or poll. The overall construction is robust, suitable for heavy-duty chopping or ceremonial display. No visible brand markings or engravings are present on the axe.
description: A double-headed axe with silver and red crescent blades on a segmented wooden handle, featuring a robust and symmetrical design.
description_view_0: Front view showing both crescent blades, with the silver blade on the left and the red blade on the right, and the full length of the wooden handle visible.
description_view_1: Side view showing the thin profile of the axe, with both blades aligned and the handle's cylindrical shape and red bands visible.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, with the red blade now on the left and the silver blade on the right, handle details still visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin edge of the blades and the handle's cylindrical form with red bands.
attribute_handle_type: Straight cylindrical wooden handle with red segmented bands, smooth surface, round cross-section, no visible curvature.
attribute_blade_design: Double crescent-shaped blades, symmetrical, broad and flat with sharp outer edges, no beard or poll, one blade silver, one blade red/orange.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with blades fitted through the handle's center.
